policiesPage.title: Policies
policiesPage.subtitle: Configure validation and enforcement rules for this workspace (Team and Business).

home.taglineSub: Document, share and track the architectural choices that matter.
home.feature1Desc: Record the context, options considered and consequences of each technical decision.
home.feature2Desc: Group decisions by project and keep everything tidy and accessible.
home.feature3Desc: Collaborate with your team and keep everyone aligned on the choices made.

hero.headline: Your decisions. Enforced in every PR.
hero.subheadline: Document what your team decided. Link it to a PR. Decern checks the code change against the decision — and blocks when it doesn't fit.

problem.title: Decisions on paper. Code that drifts.
problem.intro: Your team's decisions live in Confluence, Notion, or meeting notes. When a PR lands, nobody checks if the code still matches what you decided. With AI writing more code every day, the gap grows faster than ever.
problem.example1: A PR adds a service layer nobody agreed on. Review misses it. It ships.
problem.example2: You deprecate an old pattern. Next sprint, AI brings it back. Nobody was checking.
problem.example3: Three teams, three interpretations of the same decision. Drift compounds quietly.

solution.adrIntro: Decern uses Architecture Decision Records (ADRs): short notes that say what you decided and why. Each record follows a simple structure: context, decision, consequences. So everyone can read and update it. This format was proposed by Michael Nygard. Read the original article to learn more.
solution.title: Four steps. Two minutes to set up.
solution.step1Body: Document context, options, and the decision. Generate it from meeting notes with AI or write it manually.
solution.step2Body: Reference the decision in your PR (e.g. ADR-001). The pipeline knows which rules apply.
solution.step3Body: decern-gate runs in your pipeline (npx decern-gate). It verifies the decision exists and is approved.
solution.flowStep1Desc: Document context, options, and the decision. Generate from notes with AI or write manually.
solution.flowStep2Desc: Reference the decision in the PR. The pipeline knows which rules apply.
solution.flowStep3Desc: Gate runs in CI. Checks the decision exists and is approved.
solution.flowStep4Desc: The LLM Judge compares the diff to the decision text. Change out of scope? Gate blocks with a clear reason.
solution.step4Body: An LLM reads the decision text and the PR diff. If the change goes beyond scope or contradicts the decision, the gate blocks — even if the decision is approved. You get a clear reason and a confidence score.

judge.title: Your model. Your keys. Your threshold.
judge.intro: The Judge runs on the LLM you choose — OpenAI, Claude, or any compatible API. You set the confidence threshold. We never store your keys.
judge.point1: Pick any OpenAI-compatible provider and model. Your API key is used for that single request and never stored or logged.
judge.point2: Set a confidence threshold (default 80%). Changes above the threshold pass; below, the gate blocks. When it passes but isn't 100%, you get an advisory note on what to watch.

features.title: Built for clarity and control
features.f1Desc: Context, options, decision, consequences. One source of truth your whole team can search and reference.
features.f2Desc: Reference a decision in the PR title or description. Your CI pipeline knows which rules apply.
features.f3Desc: The Judge compares the code change to the decision. Out of scope? Gate blocks with a clear reason. No regex, just reasoning.
features.f4Desc: The Judge returns a score (0–100%) and an advisory note. Set your threshold per workspace. Know exactly how well each change aligns.
features.f5Desc: On Free, the gate reports but never blocks. Try it on real PRs with zero risk to your pipeline.
features.f6Desc: Organize decisions by project and workspace. The right people own and approve decisions.

useCases.title: Use cases
useCases.uc1Title: Tech leads who want to scale without losing control
useCases.uc1Desc: Write down the rules. The gate enforces them. You move fast without becoming the bottleneck.
useCases.uc2Title: Teams that use AI to write code
useCases.uc2Desc: AI-generated code goes through the same checks. The Judge reads the diff and the decision — no blind spots.
useCases.uc3Desc: New PRs can't bring back patterns you're removing. The gate keeps the whole team on the same direction.
useCases.uc4Desc: Show what was decided, when, and how it was enforced in CI. Clear evidence when you need it.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: How is this different from Confluence, Notion, or ADRs in Markdown?
faq.a1: Those tools store documents. They don't enforce them. Decern links decisions to PRs and runs checks in CI. If a PR breaks a decision, the pipeline can block or flag it. You also get a structured log you can search and report on.
faq.q2: How is this different from code review or static analysis?
faq.a2: Review and linters don't know your architectural decisions. Decern does. We check each PR against what you decided. One extra question: "Does this respect our decision?"
faq.q3: Does the AI write the decisions for us?
faq.a3: You can use AI to draft a decision (e.g. from meeting notes). A human reviews and approves. Decern enforces what humans decided. It doesn't replace decision-making.
faq.q4: Won't this add bureaucracy or block us too often?
faq.a4: Almost no change to your workflow: one link in the PR, one step in CI. You choose: the gate can block (strict) or only report (observation). On Free we only report, so you can try it risk-free.
faq.q5: Do we need to change our GitHub or GitLab workflow?
faq.a5: No. Add a link to the decision in the PR and one step in CI (npx decern-gate). Your process stays the same.
faq.q6: What does the Free plan include?
faq.a6: Unlimited decisions, unlimited CI checks in observation mode, AI decision generation, and LLM Judge (advisory). The gate reports but never blocks. Upgrade to Team to enable blocking and set your confidence threshold.

cta.title: Stop drift before it ships. Start in two minutes.
cta.subline: One decision log. One gate in CI. Every change checked against what you decided.

footer.copyright: © Decern. Technical decision log and CI gate for modern teams.
